title: Absent Teacher
situationText: When you arrive at school, you find out that your teacher is sick and another teacher will give the class today.

ideal.text: I ask what the class will be like and try to get to know the new teacher.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You better understand what will happen.
ideal.laterConsequence: The day goes by more smoothly.

intermediary.text: I feel uncomfortable, but I participate in the activities normally.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: It takes you a little while to adapt.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to follow most of the class.

impulsive.text: I spend the class complaining because I don't like the change.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You become more irritated during the morning.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You enjoy the proposed activities less.

title: Canceled Recess
situationText: It is raining heavily and outdoor recess has been canceled.

ideal.text: I look for another fun activity to do inside the school.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You find something interesting to do.

intermediary.text: I feel sad, but I wait for recess to end.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: Time seems to pass more slowly.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to get through the rest of the day.

impulsive.text: I say it's unfair and refuse to participate in the activities.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The rest of the period becomes more difficult.

title: Postponed Trip
situationText: The school trip that was supposed to happen today has been rescheduled to another date.

ideal.text: I ask when the new date will be and look for something positive to do today.

intermediary.text: I feel disappointed, but I continue with the day's activities.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The frustration passes gradually.

impulsive.text: I say that I don't want to participate in the trip anymore.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You react in the heat of the moment.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might regret it when you calm down.

title: Changed Classroom
situationText: Your class was moved to another room because of maintenance.

ideal.text: I explore the new space and try to organize myself.

intermediary.text: I miss the old room, but I follow the class.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You eventually get used to it.

impulsive.text: I complain all the time about the new room.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You keep focusing on the change.

title: Different Schedule
situationText: A special activity changed the normal class schedule.

ideal.text: I ask what the new schedule will be.
ideal.laterConsequence: You are better prepared for the day.

intermediary.text: I accept the change, but I feel confused for a while.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: It takes longer to understand the plan.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to follow along later.

impulsive.text: I say the day is ruined because of the change.

title: Surprise Activity
situationText: The teacher announces an activity that was not planned.

ideal.text: I listen to the instructions and try to figure out how to participate.
ideal.laterConsequence: You might end up enjoying the experience.

intermediary.text: I participate, but I still prefer the original plan.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to complete the activity.

impulsive.text: I refuse to participate because it wasn't agreed upon.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You miss out on important information.

title: New Spot in Line
situationText: Today the teacher asked everyone to change their position in the line.

ideal.text: I accept the new position and follow the class.

intermediary.text: I ask why it changed, but I accept the decision.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You are able to move forward.

impulsive.text: I insist on going back to my old spot.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You stay focused on the change.

title: Team Change
situationText: You found out that you will be in a different team than you expected for an activity.

ideal.text: I get to know the new classmates and try to collaborate.

intermediary.text: I participate, but I still prefer my previous team.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: It takes you some time to blend in.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to finish the activity.

impulsive.text: I say that I don't want to participate with different people.

title: Pair Work
situationText: The teacher drew pairs and you got a different partner than you expected.

ideal.text: I talk to my partner and we try to divide the tasks.
ideal.immediateConsequence: The activity starts in an organized way.

intermediary.text: I do my part, but I hardly talk to him/her.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The activity is completed.

impulsive.text: I say that I don't want to pair up with this person.

title: Unexpected Grade
situationText: You studied a lot, but your grade was lower than you expected.

ideal.text: I ask the teacher how I can improve.
ideal.laterConsequence: You can evolve in future activities.

intermediary.text: I feel sad, but I put the test away and move on with the day.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You remain disappointed for a while.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The emotion fades away little by little.

impulsive.text: I say the test was unfair and refuse to look at the mistakes.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss an opportunity to learn.

title: Oral Presentation
situationText: Today you need to present an assignment to the class.

ideal.text: I take a deep breath and try to present even though I feel nervous.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You realize that you can start.

intermediary.text: I present quickly to get it over with.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You still feel insecure about your performance.

impulsive.text: I refuse to present because I'm nervous.

title: Forgotten Material
situationText: You realize that you forgot the notebook needed for the activity.

ideal.text: I ask the teacher for help to find a solution.
ideal.laterConsequence: You are able to participate in the activity.

intermediary.text: I try to follow along just by observing.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You understand part of the activity.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You learn less than you could.

impulsive.text: I give up on the activity because I forgot the material.

title: Difficult Question
situationText: The teacher asks a question and you don't know the answer.

ideal.text: I say I don't know and try to learn the answer.

intermediary.text: I take a guess even without being sure.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You might discover what you got right or wrong.

impulsive.text: I get annoyed for not knowing and shut myself off from the class.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss out on important explanations.

title: Closed Library
situationText: You planned to use the library, but it is closed today.

ideal.text: I look for another place to study or read.
ideal.laterConsequence: You manage to carry out your plan.

intermediary.text: I wait without doing anything until I find another option.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You still manage to reorganize.

impulsive.text: I completely give up on the activity.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You fail to achieve your goal.

title: Redone Activity
situationText: The teacher asked you to redo the activity because some parts were missing.

ideal.text: I use the guidelines to improve my work.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You understand what needs to be fixed.

intermediary.text: I redo the activity, but I feel disappointed.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You still learn from the experience.

impulsive.text: I say I won't redo it because I already finished it once.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss an opportunity to improve.

title: New Teacher
situationText: A new teacher will start teaching your class.

ideal.text: I try to get to know the new teacher and their way of teaching.

intermediary.text: I observe first before getting used to it.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: It takes you some time to adapt.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You gradually feel more comfortable.

impulsive.text: I immediately decide that I won't like the new teacher.

title: My Friend is Playing with Someone Else
situationText: You arrive at recess and see your best friend playing with another child.

ideal.text: I ask if I can join the game.
ideal.immediateConsequence: Your friend is happy to see you.

intermediary.text: I decide to play another game at that moment.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can still talk to your friend later.

impulsive.text: I conclude that they no longer want to be my friend.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You may create a problem without understanding the situation.

title: I Wasn't Picked First
situationText: When forming teams, you were picked last.

ideal.text: I join the team and try to have fun during the game.
ideal.laterConsequence: You discover that you can still contribute to the team.

intermediary.text: I feel upset, but I continue participating.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The sadness remains for some time.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The emotion fades as the game progresses.

impulsive.text: I decide that I'm not going to play.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You are left out of the activity.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss an opportunity to have fun.

title: Who Is Right?
situationText: You and a friend disagree on a rule of the game.

ideal.text: We talk to decide together which rule to use.

intermediary.text: I accept their rule even without fully agreeing.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You keep thinking about the issue.

impulsive.text: I insist that only my rule is correct.

title: Declined Invitation
situationText: You invited a classmate to play, but they said they couldn't at that moment.

ideal.text: I ask if we can play some other time.

intermediary.text: I accept the answer and look for another activity.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You move on with your day.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation passes naturally.

impulsive.text: I believe they don't like me anymore.

title: Change of Plans
situationText: You and your friend agreed on a game, but they want to do something else.

ideal.text: I suggest playing a little bit of each activity.

intermediary.text: I accept their idea, even though I prefer mine.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You would still have liked to express your opinion.

impulsive.text: I decide not to play anymore.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: The activity ends before it begins.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss an opportunity for fun.

title: Another Idea Was Chosen
situationText: Your group chose a different game than the one you suggested.

ideal.text: I participate in the group's choice and observe how the game will be.
ideal.laterConsequence: You might end up liking the activity.

intermediary.text: I participate, but I still prefer my idea.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You follow along with the activity.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You still enjoy part of the experience.

impulsive.text: I say the game is bad and leave the group.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You distance yourself from your classmates.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss the opportunity to participate.

title: Waiting for a Friend
situationText: Your friend took longer than expected to arrive.

ideal.text: I wait a little and try to understand what happened.
ideal.laterConsequence: You discover there was a reason for the delay.

intermediary.text: I feel annoyed, but I keep waiting.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation is resolved when they arrive.

impulsive.text: I leave immediately without asking for the reason.

title: A New Game
situationText: Your classmates want to play something you have never tried.

ideal.text: I ask them to explain the rules and try to participate.

intermediary.text: I observe first before deciding to participate.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can decide later with more confidence.

impulsive.text: I say I don't want to because it's something unknown.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss the opportunity to try something new.

title: I Lost the Match
situationText: You were very excited to win the game, but ended up losing.

ideal.text: I congratulate whoever won and think about what I learned during the game.
ideal.laterConsequence: You continue to enjoy the activity.

intermediary.text: I feel disappointed, but I continue participating.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You need some time to accept the result.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The frustration diminishes.

impulsive.text: I say the game was unfair because I lost.

title: New Rule
situationText: Before the match started, the group decided to use a different rule than usual.

ideal.text: I ask them to explain the rule and try to play that way.
ideal.laterConsequence: You may discover new ways to play.

intermediary.text: I accept the rule, but I still prefer the old one.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to follow the game.

impulsive.text: I refuse to play because I don't like the change.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You are left out of the activity.

title: Picked Last
situationText: During team formation, you were picked last.

ideal.text: I join the team and try to do my best.
ideal.laterConsequence: You discover that you can contribute to the group.

intermediary.text: I feel upset, but I keep playing.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The emotion remains for some time.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You still enjoy part of the activity.

impulsive.text: I decide not to participate anymore.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss the opportunity to play.

title: On Another Team
situationText: You were put on a different team than your friends.

ideal.text: I get to know the classmates on my new team and participate in the game.

intermediary.text: I play normally, but I miss my friends.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to enjoy the activity.

impulsive.text: I ask to switch teams or I don't play.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You miss part of the experience.

title: Interrupted Game
situationText: The game was fun, but it had to end earlier than expected.

ideal.text: I ask when we can continue the game.
ideal.laterConsequence: The interruption becomes easier to accept.

intermediary.text: I feel disappointed, but I accept the ending.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to move on to the next activity.

impulsive.text: I insist that no one should stop now.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: A conflict arises with the group.

title: Occupied Toy
situationText: You want to use a very popular toy, but another child is using it.

ideal.text: I wait my turn or look for another activity in the meantime.
ideal.laterConsequence: You will have the opportunity to play later.

intermediary.text: I ask how much time is left.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The wait becomes more predictable.

impulsive.text: I demand that the child leave immediately.

title: Long Line
situationText: There are many children waiting for the same activity.

ideal.text: I wait my turn or temporarily choose another activity.

intermediary.text: I wait, but I feel impatient.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You still manage to participate.

impulsive.text: I try to cut in front of the other children.

title: That Wasn't Fair
situationText: During the game, you notice that a classmate did not follow the agreed-upon rules.

ideal.text: I calmly talk about what happened and try to clarify the rules.
ideal.immediateConsequence: The group is able to discuss the situation.
ideal.laterConsequence: The game is more likely to continue.

intermediary.text: I keep playing, even though I think it's unfair.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You might remain bothered by what happened.

impulsive.text: I accuse the classmate aggressively and abandon the game.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The activity may end for everyone.

title: I Made a Mistake
situationText: You answered a question in class and realized that your answer was wrong.

ideal.text: I accept the mistake and pay attention to learn the correct answer.

intermediary.text: I feel embarrassed, but I continue following the class.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The discomfort lingers for some time.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The emotion fades naturally.

impulsive.text: I decide not to participate in class anymore that day.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You distance yourself from the activity.

title: Too Much Noise
situationText: The environment is noisier than usual and it is bothering you.

ideal.text: I look for a strategy to calm down or ask an adult for help.
ideal.laterConsequence: You are able to continue participating.

intermediary.text: I try to ignore the noise and continue the activity.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You might manage to finish the task.

impulsive.text: I start complaining or yelling for everyone to stop.

title: It's Not My Turn Yet
situationText: You want to participate in the activity, but you have to wait for other children to finish first.

ideal.text: I find something to do while I wait.
ideal.laterConsequence: You approach the activity feeling calmer.

intermediary.text: I wait, but I get quite impatient.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You still manage to participate.

impulsive.text: I try to interrupt those who are participating.

title: Different Plan
situationText: You had an idea for an activity, but it didn't work out as you imagined.

ideal.text: I think of another way to solve the problem.

intermediary.text: I feel frustrated, but I keep trying.
intermediary.laterConsequence: There is still a chance for success.

impulsive.text: I immediately give up on the activity.

title: A Suggestion for Improvement
situationText: An adult explained how you could improve something you did.

ideal.text: I listen to the guidance and think about what I can learn.

intermediary.text: I feel bothered, but I agree to listen.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You utilize part of the guidance.

impulsive.text: I interpret the correction as a personal insult.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You lose an opportunity for growth.

title: Everything Changed
situationText: A highly anticipated activity had to be changed at the last minute.

ideal.text: I ask what the new available options are.

intermediary.text: I am quite disappointed, but I go along with the new plan.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The frustration lingers for some time.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You are able to participate in the activity.

impulsive.text: I decide that nothing else is worth it that day.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You enjoy the available opportunities less.

title: I Was Left Out
situationText: You find out that some classmates did an activity together and you didn't participate.

ideal.text: I try to better understand the situation before drawing conclusions.
ideal.laterConsequence: You might discover explanations you hadn't imagined.

intermediary.text: I feel sad, but I wait before talking about it.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You are able to reflect more calmly.

impulsive.text: I immediately conclude that no one likes me.

title: I Did It!
situationText: You achieved a very good result in an important activity.

ideal.text: I celebrate my achievement and acknowledge the effort I put in.
ideal.laterConsequence: Your confidence grows in a healthy way.

intermediary.text: I am very happy and want to tell everyone immediately.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You remain proud of the achievement.

impulsive.text: I start belittling classmates who had a harder time.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Your success may generate unnecessary conflicts.
